# Perfect Number Theory of the Standard Model -- Executive Brief

## The Claim

The Standard Model particle spectrum is uniquely determined by the arithmetic of perfect number 6. The identity R(n) = sigma(n) phi(n) / (n tau(n)) = 1 has exactly two solutions: n=1 (trivial) and n=6. All 10 Standard Model particle counts, the gauge group decomposition, and fermion mass ratios emerge from n=6 divisor arithmetic with zero free parameters.

## Key Evidence

- **10/10 particle counts exact**: quarks=6=
